items.headline: Knicks Eye Commanding 3-0 Lead in NBA Finals Game 3 Tonight!
items.summary: The New York Knicks are set to host the San Antonio Spurs in Game 3 of the NBA Finals tonight at Madison Square Garden, holding a dominant 2-0 series advantage. New York is just two wins away from their first championship since 1973, with tickets reportedly soaring to unprecedented prices as 'Knicks fever' grips the city.

items.headline: Blockbuster Trade Rumors Swirl: Jaylen Brown to Hawks, Giannis Future Uncertain
items.summary: Speculation is mounting around potential seismic shifts across the league. A proposed three-team trade could send Celtics star Jaylen Brown to the Atlanta Hawks, while the future of two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo with the Milwaukee Bucks remains a hot topic, with the Miami Heat reportedly emerging as frontrunners amidst doubts about a move to Boston.

items.headline: No NBA Games Scheduled
items.summary: There were no NBA games played on Sunday, June 7, 2026. The league is preparing for Game 3 of the NBA Finals tonight.

items.headline: Flashback: Knicks Edge Spurs in Thrilling Game 2 on Friday!
items.summary: In the last game played, on Friday, June 5, the New York Knicks secured a nail-biting 105-104 victory over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 2 of the NBA Finals. Jalen Brunson hit the decisive free throw, while Karl-Anthony Towns dominated the boards.

items.headline: NBA Finals Game 3: Spurs at Knicks
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs travel to Madison Square Garden to face the New York Knicks in a pivotal Game 3 of the NBA Finals. The Knicks lead the series 2-0. Tip-off is scheduled for 8:30 PM ET.

items.headline: Brunson Delivers Clutch in Game 2
items.summary: Jalen Brunson spearheaded the Knicks' Game 2 victory with 20 points, including the go-ahead free throw in the final seconds. He also dropped 30 points in Game 1, with 13 coming in the fourth quarter.

items.headline: KAT Dominates Glass in Game 2
items.summary: Karl-Anthony Towns was a force for the Knicks in Game 2, contributing 21 points, a massive 13 rebounds, and 4 assists.

items.headline: Wemby's Playoff Dominance
items.summary: Despite the Spurs' 0-2 deficit, Victor Wembanyama has been a statistical marvel in the playoffs, averaging 23.6 points, 10.7 rebounds, and an astounding 3.53 blocks per game.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Play-In Masterclass
items.summary: Israeli standout Deni Avdija had a career-defining performance in the Play-In Tournament, erupting for 41 points, 12 assists, 7 rebounds, and 2 blocks to lead the Blazers to the playoffs. He also averaged over 25 points per game during the regular season.

items.headline: Mitchell Robinson Cleared for Game 3 Despite Hand Fracture
items.summary: Knicks center Mitchell Robinson has been cleared to play in Game 3 of the NBA Finals against the Spurs, despite suffering a fractured right 5th metacarpal.

items.headline: Donte DiVincenzo Out for Postseason with Torn Achilles
items.summary: In unfortunate news, Donte DiVincenzo has been diagnosed with a torn right Achilles tendon and will miss the remainder of the postseason.

items.headline: Cavs Held Firm on Mobley, Refused Giannis Trade
items.summary: The Cleveland Cavaliers reportedly made the strategic decision to refuse to trade Evan Mobley for Giannis Antetokounmpo, signaling their long-term commitment to Mobley.

items.headline: Rui Hachimura to Clippers in Proposed Three-Team Trade
items.summary: A new trade idea suggests Rui Hachimura could be on the move, swapping the Lakers for the Clippers in a three-team deal.
